title: MENTAL HEALTH REFERRALS - 211info
text: HILLSBORO RECOVERY CENTER MENTAL HEALTH REFERRALS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an information and referral line to help find mental health providers. County behavioral health services are provided through a network of community agencies. 5250 NE Elam Young Parkway Hillsboro, OR 97124 Eligibility: Medicaid recipients and uninsured patients seeking mental health services Hours: Support Team: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Crisis Line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 846-4528 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 846-4528 Behavioral Health Support Team Helpline (503) 291-9111 Crisis Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call or visit website FEES: Accepts OHP. No cost for uninsured patients. DOCUMENTS: Insurance information, if applicable LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, interpretation or signing by arrangement AREA SERVED: Washington County TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Next to Hawthorn Farm TriMet MAX stop Services: Specialized Information and Referral * Mental Health Issues Therapy Referrals * Mental Health Issues Other Services Offered At This Location: INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - WRAPAROUND CARE COORDINATION MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - CIVIL COMMITMENT PROGRAM MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - WASHINGTON COUNTY CRISIS LINE MENTAL HEALTH CARE FACILITIES - HAWTHORN WALK-IN CENTER SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SERVICES - ALCOHOL, DRUG, AND GAMBLING ADDICTION SERVICES SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SERVICES - CENTER FOR ADDICTIONS TRIAGE AND TREATMENT If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. HILLSBORO RECOVERY CENTER
